:WATER 
DISTRICT
Direciors
Bette Boalmun
President

Easlern Contra Costa County
May 15, l-990
Kathryn Donovan, Esq., General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 958L4
RE: Request for Advice - Political Expenditures
Dear Ms. Donovan:
This letter Serves as a request for formal written adwice
regarding whether or not Specific expenditures of funds for
two communications by the Contra Costa Water District would
be considered political 
expenditures.
The Contra Costa Viater District is a public agency governed
by an elected S-member board. This District's
responsibility is to maintain, enhance and provide safe
water at the lowest possible cost to its customers. Central
to our long ranqe planing has been the construction of Los
Vaqueros Reservoir, a major project which will Provide a new
facility 
for water storage.
we have a longstanding customer relations program in which
the District communicates with its customers on a regular
basis. For example, Lhe District often includes
informational inserts in billing 
statements sent to our
customers. We also publish a quarterly newsletter, the
purpose of which is to inform customers of our activities.
The District also maintains an ongoing speaker's bureau in
which a speaker makes presentations before civic groups and
associations. We provide a video and/or slide show
illustrating 
the District's 
activities to accompany the
-!r.

or

-

@

Page 2
Kathryn Donovan, Esq., General Counsel
May l-5, 1990
presentation. we have also communicated to our customers by
radio and magazine advertisements to educate our customers
about our activities.
The content of all these communications is of an educational
and informational- naLure to describe to our customers our
efforts to provide yrater quality.
Specifically at issue j-s our need to update our video
(script and. video enclosed) . we have been presenting
video/slide show presentations since 1986. APproximately
every L-1/2 years, we update the video and slides to refl-ect
our current activities. 
This video, "CCWD: Los Vaqueros'r,
was produced in 1988 and has been in use since then. In
this vj-deo, there is significant discussion of the Los
vaqueros Reservoir project since its construction is crucial
in our program to defiver water of high guality.
About six months ago, the Dj.strict began work on a new
script to update its video program. A copy of this new
video script is enclosed. About the time the script was
approved for production, citizens of the District began
circulating petitions to place an initiative 
on the ba1lot
which, if enacted, would require the Contra Costa Water
Distric! to withdrat from completing the Los Vaqueros
Reservoir project (copy enclosed) . Presently the petition
is still 
circulating.
The proposed video is part of the District, s normal public
relations process and is in no way intended to influence the
qualification of the proposed initiative 
nor the final vote.


Page 3
Kathryn Donovan, Esq., Generaf Counsel
May 1"5, 1990
we bring our question to you because the initiative 
asks the
voters to reject a specific project which is integral and
necessary to the District's 
ongoing programs and future
pfans. we cannot do a video without mentioning Los
Vaqueros. But we need your agreement that this mention does
not make this video a pol,itical expenditure.
The same question arises in the communication entitled "ON
TAP " (enctosed), which is a periodic mailing by the District
and illustrates the type of communication we send to our
customers each calendar quarter. Again, the same question
arises whether either this letter and attachments will be
construed as a political 
expenditure.
It is our understanding that the regulations of the Eair
Po1itical Practices Commission define an expenditure as any
payment made for political purposes. They say that payment
is made for political 
purposes if it is for the Purpose of
influencing or attempting to influence the action of the
voters for or against the qualifications or passage of any
measure. we did not produce this video to influence or
attempt to influence the voters since its entire content lras
conceived and developed without any knowledge that such an
initiative 
was going to be circulated. 
Nor is there any
mention that there may be a ballot measure on the Los
Vaqueros project. Also, we believe that all references to
Los vaqueros in "ON TA.P" are factual, and the communication
does not advocate, either directly or indirectly, a position
against the initiative, 
which may or may not ever qualify.
Afthough rde are not advocating or trying to influence the
voters, we are concerned that any supportive mention of Los
Vaqueros wj-11 be construed to be a political 
expenditure.


Page 4
Kathryn Donovan, Esq., General Counsel
May 15, 19 90
Because we are mindful of the prohibition against using
public funds for elections, rre want to be very cautious so
that we do not make a political 
expenditure as defined by
laws you interpret. 
Thank you for your prompt consideration
of this matter.
S incerel-y,
Andrew C. .lohnson
Assistant to Generaf Manager,
Governmental Relations

sections.question: Whether specific expenditures of funds for two communications by the Contra Costa Water District—an updated video titled 'CCWD: Los Vaqueros' and a periodic mailing entitled 'ON TAP'—would be considered political expenditures under FPPC regulations, given that these communications discuss the Los Vaqueros Reservoir project which is the subject of a pending ballot initiative seeking to prevent the project's completion.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the Contra Costa Water District's planned video and newsletter communications about the Los Vaqueros Reservoir project constitute political expenditures under FPPC law, even though the materials were conceived before knowledge of the opposing initiative and do not explicitly advocate for or against the ballot measure?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a public agency to the FPPC seeking advice, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present in this document. The question section is extensive and detailed. The document clearly articulates the legal inquiry regarding political expenditure classification.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether specific expenditures of funds for two communications by the Contra Costa Water District—an updated video titled 'CCWD: Los Vaqueros' and a periodic mailing entitled 'ON TAP'—would be considered political expenditures under FPPC regulations, given that these communications discuss the Los Vaqueros Reservoir project which is the subject of a pending ballot initiative seeking to prevent the project's completion.

embedding.summary: The Contra Costa Water District requests formal written advice from the FPPC regarding whether two planned communications—an updated video and a quarterly newsletter—discussing the Los Vaqueros Reservoir project constitute political expenditures, given that citizens have begun circulating a petition to place a ballot initiative opposing the project.
